title: pleasingly paprika norwegian root stew
description: a hearty, whole30-friendly stew featuring smoky paprika, resinous celery root, and chewy turnips. perfect for a cold norwegian winter's night.
instructions: heat the donabe over medium heat on your stovetop, or warm it in your oven at 375f (190c).
add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
add the diced celery root and turnips, smoked paprika, caraway seeds, kosher salt, and black pepper. stir to combine.
pour in the beef broth and bring the stew to a simmer.
transfer the donabe to the oven and braise the stew for 30 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
remove the donabe from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs or a dollop of sour cream if desired.
discard any leftover bones or solids before serving.
serve with your favorite sides, such as crusty bread or a green salad.
store any leftover stew in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, or freeze for up to 2 months.
reheat the stew over low heat, or in the microwave, until warmed through.
